task_completion.description: Verify the collection 'Week 1 - Judicial Review' is created in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Week 1 - Judicial Review' in the left sidebar or collection pane of the Jurism/Zotero interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the case 'Marbury v. Madison' is present in the 'Week 1 - Judicial Review' collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Marbury v. Madison' in the main item list when the 'Week 1 - Judicial Review' collection is selected.

task_completion.description: Verify the article 'Constitutional Fact Review' is present in the 'Week 1 - Judicial Review' collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Constitutional Fact Review' in the main item list alongside the Marbury case.

task_completion.description: Verify a child note is attached to 'Marbury v. Madison' containing the required text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an expanded view under 'Marbury v. Madison' showing a note icon, or the note editor pane on the right containing the text 'Focus on Marshall's reasoning regarding original jurisdiction vs appellate jurisdiction.'

task_completion.description: Verify the collection report was exported as an HTML file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window showing 'week1_syllabus.html' in the '/home/ga/Documents' directory, a save dialog confirming this path, or a browser/HTML viewer displaying the generated report.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Jurism GUI to generate the report rather than writing a mock HTML file via terminal or text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Jurism 'Generate Report' dialog, right-click context menus in Jurism, or the Save As dialog, rather than terminal commands like 'echo' or text editors.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually interacted with Jurism to create the collection and add items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the Jurism application open with the items being searched, dragged, or edited, rather than just a final file manager view.

integrity.description: Ensure the child note was created inside Jurism's note editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The note text should appear in the right-hand pane of Jurism with the rich text formatting toolbar visible, not in a standalone text editor like gedit or nano.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a collection named 'Week 1 - Judicial Review' in Jurism. It must contain two specific items: (1) 'Marbury v. Madison' (a 1803 Supreme Court case, 5 U.S. 137) and (2) 'Constitutional Fact Review' (a 1985 article by Henry P. Monaghan). A child note must be attached to 'Marbury v. Madison' containing the exact text: 'Focus on Marshall's reasoning regarding original jurisdiction vs appellate jurisdiction.' Finally, an HTML report of this collection must be exported to '/home/ga/Documents/week1_syllabus.html'.
